steps.title: Apply the discriminant condition for no real solutions
steps.content:
For the equation $-x^2 + bx - 169 = 0$, we identify $a = -1$, linear coefficient is $b$, and $c = -169$. For no real solutions, the discriminant must be negative. Applying this:
$$b^2 - 4(-1)(-169) < 0$$
$$b^2 - 676 < 0$$

steps.title: Solve the inequality for b
steps.content:
From $b^2 - 676 < 0$, we get:
$$b^2 < 676$$
Since $676 = 26^2$, this becomes:
$$b^2 < 26^2$$
Taking square roots:
$$|b| < 26$$
Which means:
$$-26 < b < 26$$

steps.title: Find the least integer value of b
steps.content: Since $b$ must be an integer with $-26-25.
